name: Visual Analog Scale for Pain
description: A unidimensional measure of pain intensity widely used in clinical and research settings. Can be administered using multiple formats including numeric rating, word descriptors, visual analog line, or graphic faces scale.
recallPeriod: Current or past week (specify when administering)
instructions: Rate your pain intensity using one of the following scale formats. Select the number, word, or position on the line that best describes your current pain level.

numericRating.name: Numeric Rating Scale (NRS)
numericRating.description: 0-10 numeric scale where 0 = no pain and 10 = worst possible pain

wordDescriptor.name: Word Descriptor Scale
wordDescriptor.description: Verbal descriptors anchored at key points

visualAnalog.name: Visual Analog Scale (VAS)
visualAnalog.description: 100mm horizontal line with anchors at each end
visualAnalog.measurement: Distance in mm from left anchor (0-100)

facesScale.name: Graphic Faces Scale
facesScale.description: Color-coded series of faces showing increasing levels of distress
facesScale.instruction: Rate your pain or point to the face that describes how you are feeling

questions.question: On a scale of 0 to 10, with 0 meaning no pain and 10 meaning the worst pain you can imagine, how much pain have you had?

severityBands.label: Mild Pain
severityBands.description: Pain is present but does not limit activity.

severityBands.label: Moderate Pain
severityBands.description: Pain limits some activities. May require treatment.

severityBands.label: Severe Pain
severityBands.description: Significant pain that severely limits function. Requires intervention.

interpretation.scoringMethod: Single score from 0-10 (NRS) or 0-100mm (VAS line). For VAS line, measure distance from left anchor in millimeters.
interpretation.clinicallyMeaningfulChange: A change of 2 points (or 20mm on VAS) is generally considered clinically meaningful.
interpretation.formatsEquivalence: NRS 0-10 and VAS 0-100mm are generally considered equivalent when VAS is divided by 10.
interpretation.clinicalUse: Used for pain assessment, treatment monitoring, and outcome measurement. Can be used for various pain types by specifying body location in the question.
interpretation.populationConsiderations: Faces scale may be preferred for children, elderly, or patients with cognitive impairment.

references.citation: Hawker GA, Mian S, Kendzerska T, French M. Measures of adult pain: Visual Analog Scale for Pain (VAS Pain), Numeric Rating Scale for Pain (NRS Pain), McGill Pain Questionnaire (MPQ), Short-Form McGill Pain Questionnaire (SF-MPQ), Chronic Pain Grade Scale (CPGS), Short Form-36 Bodily Pain Scale (SF-36 BPS), and Measure of Intermittent and Constant Osteoarthritis Pain (ICOAP). Arthritis Care Res. 2011;63 Suppl 11:S240-52.
Jensen MP, Karoly P, Braver S. The measurement of clinical pain intensity: a comparison of six methods. Pain. 1986;27(1):117-126.
